name: seo-forge
title: SEO Forge
description: Agent-portable skill for evidence-based SEO content work: keyword and SERP research, competitor analysis, brand-knowledge constraints, template-driven drafting, E-E-A-T scoring, optimization, editorial gates, technical SEO validation, publishing, deployment verification, and install/export across agent runtimes.

required_capabilities.name: file_read
required_capabilities.purpose: Read configs, templates, generated articles, and target-site source files.

required_capabilities.name: file_write
required_capabilities.purpose: Write configs, drafts, scoring reports, and publish-ready article files.

required_capabilities.name: shell
required_capabilities.purpose: Run the zero-dependency CLI, tests, git commands, and platform build checks.

required_capabilities.name: web_search
required_capabilities.purpose: Discover keyword trends, competitors, SERP signals, and reference sources.

required_capabilities.name: web_fetch
required_capabilities.purpose: Read competitor pages and verify reference URLs.

optional_capabilities.name: repo_read
optional_capabilities.purpose: Inspect remote GitHub repositories when the target site is not already local.

optional_capabilities.name: pull_request
optional_capabilities.purpose: Open PRs and merge after checks when the host agent supports repository operations.

optional_capabilities.name: deployment_checks
optional_capabilities.purpose: Monitor GitHub Actions, Vercel, or another deployment surface after publishing.

optional_capabilities.name: image_generation
optional_capabilities.purpose: Create or verify article images when visual content is required.

codex.notes: Install the bundle into the Codex skill directory and rely on native web, shell, git, and GitHub/Vercel plugin tools where available.

claude_code.notes: Install the bundle into the Claude skills directory. GLM MCP names are supported by default but can be remapped.

opencode.notes: Install the bundle into the runtime skill directory and point MCP capabilities to GLM, Tavily, Exa, Fetch, or GitHub tools.

generic.notes: Expose the capabilities listed in agent_contract and map them with templates/agent-capabilities.json.
